mnemonics.title: Merge Sort Steps
mnemonics.content: D.I.C.E - Divide the array, Invoke recursively on halves, Combine sorted halves, End when sorted

acronyms.expansion: Divide the array into two halves
acronyms.usage: First phase of merge sort where array is split into subarrays
Second phase where each subarray is sorted recursively
Third phase where merge operation produces sorted array

visualTips.visualization: Draw an inverted tree: root is full array, each level splits into 2 branches, leaves are single elements. There are log₂n levels.
Think of two sorted decks of cards being merged by comparing top cards - always pick the smaller one.
Picture breaking a large pizza into individual slices (divide), eating each slice (conquer), and then stacking them back (combine).

analogies.analogy: Like organizing books by floor: divide books into piles by floor, sort each pile, then stack all first floors together, second floors together, etc.
In a classroom, students are already lined up by height. When re-sorting by weight, students with same weight maintain their original height order - that's stability.
Think of it like searching in a balanced binary tree: at each of log n levels, you process n elements.
